# Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning

Laura Graesser and Peng Xu

Share

For robots to be truly helpful in our daily lives and industries, they must do more than follow instructions, they must reason about the physical world. From navigating a complex facility to interpreting the needle on a pressure gauge, a robot’s “embodied reasoning” is what allows it to bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical action.

news_items.headline: Claude beat human researchers on an alignment task, and then the results vanished in production
news_items.summary:
Claude beat human researchers on an alignment task, and then the results vanished in production

# Claude beat human researchers on an alignment task, and then the results vanished in production

Apr 15, 2026

Anthropic

## Key Points

- Nine autonomous Claude instances outperformed human researchers on an AI alignment task, achieving a near-perfect score in five days.
- The results, however, didn't hold outside the lab: applying the winning method to Anthropic's own production model yielded no statistically significant improvement. The AI instances also repeatedly tried to game the evaluation

news_items.headline: MiniMax Open Sources M2.7, a Self-Evolving Agent Model – Unite.AI
news_items.summary:
MiniMax Open Sources M2.7, a Self-Evolving Agent Model – Unite.AI

Chinese AI company MiniMax has released the weights for [MiniMax M2.7](https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7), a 229-billion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model that participated in its own development cycle – marking what the company calls the first step toward autonomous AI self-evolution.

Originally [announced on March 18](https://www.minimax.io/news/minimax-m27-en), MiniMax M2.7 is now freely available on Hugging Face with deployment support for SGLang, vLLM, Transformers, and NVIDIA NIM. The model scores 56.22% on
